summary: PR 2488 split the previous sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34 range into hsd_3B34 and new hsd_3B5C translation units, added large nonmatching C bodies for JPEG encode/decode routines, updated split metadata and configure.py, and reported fuzzy progress plus data/rodata matches. The PR body explicitly justifies the TU split at hsd_803B5C4C using adjacent extab/extabindex cut points, separated data clusters, hsd_803B5C2C matching at 100%, and a rebuilt sections/link check.

key_files.role: Split metadata changed: hsd_3B34 text/extab/extabindex/data/sdata2 ranges were shortened and a new hsd_3B5C TU range was added starting at text 0x803B5C4C.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: config/GALE01/splits.txt added 12 deleted 5
raw_diff: config/GALE01/splits.txt hsd_3B34 range changed and hsd_3B5C.c split added
pr_body: TU split rationale

raw_diff: Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c") added

key_files.role: Expanded first TU with nonmatching JPEG encode-side routines, JPEG tables/data, externs, includes, M2C_FIELD-heavy generated-looking code, and retained hsd_803B5C2C in the first cluster per PR body.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: hsd_3B34.c added 1798 deleted 5
raw_diff: hsd_3B34.c adds hsd_803B3408 and static JPEG table clusters
pr_body: hsd_3B34 fuzzy 84.855255%; hsd_803B5C2C matching at 100%

pr_body verification lists hsd_3B34.h in pre-commit files

key_files.role: New nonmatching TU for the second JPEG/decode-side helper cluster beginning at hsd_803B5C4C.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: hsd_3B5C.c added 1058
raw_diff: new split entry hsd_3B5C.c text 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230, data 0x80431090-0x804316C0, sdata2 0x804DEB90-0x804DEC00
pr_body: hsd_3B5C fuzzy 84.95146%

what_changed: Split sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c into two TUs: hsd_3B34.c now ends at text 0x803B5C4C and new hsd_3B5C.c covers text 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230.
Adjusted extab/extabindex boundaries at 0x80005690 and 0x800058E8, and data/sdata2 boundaries at 0x80431090 and 0x804DEB90, respectively.
Added hsd_3B5C.c to configure.py as Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B5C.c").
Added nonmatching C for JPEG encode/decode-related routines in both TUs, with many address-style symbols, static table blobs, extern globals, and generated-style temporaries.
PR body claims hsd_803B5C2C stayed matching at 100%, hsd_3B34 reached 84.855255% fuzzy, and hsd_3B5C reached 84.95146% fuzzy.
Bot report showed three new data/rodata matches and 15 fuzzy improvements, including .data for both hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C at 100% and .rodata for hsd_3B34 at 100%.
smart_moves: The TU split was justified with multiple independent section-boundary signals rather than only a text function boundary: adjacent extab/extabindex cut points, clean data-section separation, and separate .sdata2 constants.
Keeping hsd_803B5C2C at the end of the first cluster because it matched 100% immediately before hsd_803B5C4C gave a concrete anchor for the proposed boundary.
The PR separated the new hsd_3B5C object in configure.py and splits.txt instead of only adding code to the existing hsd_3B34 file.
Verification listed both source-format/pre-commit and full configure+ninja rebuild steps.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When splitting a TU, use aligned evidence from text, extab/extabindex, data, and sdata2 ranges before treating a function address as a boundary.
evidence_refs: pr_body: split at hsd_803B5C4C looked like natural TU boundary; clean adjacent extab/extabindex cut points; data sections separated cleanly
raw_diff: splits.txt moves extab/extabindex/data/sdata2 endpoints and creates hsd_3B5C.c ranges

decomp_lessons.lesson: A fully matching small routine at the end of a data/function cluster can be useful boundary evidence when the next routine begins a different helper cluster, but should be combined with section metadata.
evidence_refs: pr_body: hsd_803B5C2C behaved like a small end-of-first-cluster routine and matched 100% before hsd_803B5C4C starts decode-side helper cluster

decomp_lessons.lesson: For large nonmatching JPEG-style code intake, landing address-style symbols and generated temporaries can be acceptable as an initial nonmatching/fuzzy progress step, but should not be treated as final authored style without later cleanup.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: many fn_/lbl_/hsd_ address-style symbols, temp_rXX/var_rXX locals, M2C_FIELD uses
pr_body: add nonmatching C; fuzzy scores below 100%

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven static/local routines and data remained address-style, e.g. fn_803B376C, lbl_803B9670, lbl_80430C40, and hsd_803B3408.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_3B34.c function/data declarations use fn_/lbl_/address names

naming_conventions.convention: Generated temporaries such as temp_r22, var_r10, and unused_arg4 appear in the nonmatching code and should be considered bridge names, not a semantic naming standard.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: hsd_803B3408 locals include temp_r22, var_r10, unused_arg4
decomp_standards: conservative-naming says clean temp_rXX names once a local role is clear

raw_diff: splits.txt exact range changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use section metadata to justify TU splits
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2488 supports a reusable lesson that TU splits should be justified with aligned text, extab/extabindex, data, and sdata2 boundary evidence, not only a chosen function address. Here the split at hsd_803B5C4C was justified by adjacent extab/extabindex cut points, separated data clusters, and separate .sdata2 constants.
evidence_refs: pr_body: TU split rationale
raw_diff: splits.txt range changes

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use matched end-of-cluster routine as boundary anchor with corroborating metadata
accepted_candidate_records.text: A small fully matching routine at the end of one cluster can help anchor a TU boundary when the next address starts a different helper cluster, but this PR's evidence is strongest because that observation was combined with section metadata.
evidence_refs: pr_body: hsd_803B5C2C matched 100% before hsd_803B5C4C decode-side helper cluster

source_update_candidates.title: sysdolphin/baselib hsd_3B34 to hsd_3B5C split boundary
source_update_candidates.text: In PR 2488, hsd_3B34.c was split so hsd_3B34 covers text 0x803B3408-0x803B5C4C and hsd_3B5C covers text 0x803B5C4C-0x803B7230. Related section boundaries in the diff are extab 0x80005690, extabindex 0x800058E8, data 0x80431090, and sdata2 0x804DEB90.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: config/GALE01/splits.txt hsd_3B34 and hsd_3B5C ranges
pr_body: TU split rationale
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful as a source-owned path fact candidate for future searches around this TU boundary; curator should verify against current splits before promoting.

source_update_candidates.title: TU split evidence should combine exception and data section boundaries
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding a standard note that TU-split PRs should document section-boundary evidence such as extab/extabindex, .data, .rodata, and .sdata2 ownership, especially when creating a new object for a partially nonmatching range.
evidence_refs: pr_body: clean adjacent extab/extabindex cut points and separated data sections; rebuilt sections/link check corroborated split
raw_diff: splits.txt exact split ranges
source_update_candidates.reason: This PR provides a concrete example aligned with existing data-sections-and-tu-splits and verification standards; curator should decide whether to fold it into an existing standard rather than create a new one.

source_update_candidates.title: JPEG state structure cleanup candidate for hsd_804D2648 buffer accesses
source_update_candidates.text: The added nonmatching code accesses hsd_804D2648 through HSD_804D2648_BUF and M2C_FIELD offsets such as 0x518 and 0x618. Future decomp work may need a recovered state/buffer type for these offsets.
evidence_refs: raw_diff: HSD_804D2648_BUF definition and M2C_FIELD uses
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a cleanup/search candidate only; the PR does not prove field names or structure layout.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the exact fuzzy percentages as current validation facts without checking current objdiff/build output; they are historical PR/body and bot-report evidence.
Do not treat temp_rXX/var_rXX names, M2C_FIELD offsets, or raw table labels as accepted naming/style standards; they appear in nonmatching bridge code.
Do not infer semantic JPEG table names or state-field names from the raw byte arrays alone; the PR keeps address-style labels and provides no naming proof.
Do not infer human reviewer approval details beyond the PR being merged; no human review comments are loaded.
